Title: A Novel WRKY Transcription Factor from Ipomoea trifida, ItfWRKY70, Confers Drought Tolerance in Sweet Potato

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: ItfWRKY70 is a 304-amino-acid protein (calculated MW ~33.9 kDa, pI 7.57) encoded by a gene with three exons and two introns; it shares highest sequence identity with a WRKY70 from Ipomoea nil and lower identity with WRKY70 proteins from Nicotiana tomentosiformis, Vitis vinifera and Arabidopsis thaliana. The C-terminal region of ItfWRKY70 is required for self-transcriptional activation in yeast, whereas deletion mutants lacking the appropriate C-terminal sequence lose transactivation activity. The promoter region contains multiple stress-related cis-elements (e.g., LTR, ARE, GARE, CGTCA-motif), consistent with its induction by abiotic stress and ABA.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression of ItfWRKY70 in sweet potato markedly enhances drought tolerance by improving plant growth and biomass under PEG-induced and soil-based drought, increasing ABA and proline accumulation, elevating SOD and POD activities, reducing H2O2 and MDA levels, and decreasing stomatal aperture and water loss under drought stress
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Acts as a nuclear WRKY transcription factor whose C-terminal region functions as a transcriptional activation domain in yeast; overexpression elevates transcription of ABA biosynthesis genes (NCED, AAO/AAO3), osmolyte biosynthesis and LEA genes (P5CS, LEA/LEA5), ROS-scavenging genes (SOD, POD, CAT), and stomatal-movement regulators (SLAC1, OST1/SnRK2.6) in transgenic sweet potato under drought stress
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Participates in ABA-dependent drought stress responses by promoting ABA biosynthesis and ABA-responsive gene expression, activates ROS-scavenging pathways, and modulates stomatal movement pathways involved in water-loss control
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: In transgenic sweet potato under drought stress, overexpression of ItfWRKY70 leads to activation (upregulated transcript levels) of NCED and AAO/AAO3 (ABA biosynthesis), P5CS and LEA/LEA5 (osmotic adjustment), SOD, POD and CAT (ROS scavenging), and IbSLAC1 and IbOST1/SnRK2.6 (stomatal movement regulators)
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: WRKY transcription factor, group III WRKY containing a conserved WRKYGQK domain and a C2HC-type zinc-finger motif
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus, demonstrated by ItfWRKY70-GFP fusion localized to nuclei in Nicotiana benthamiana leaf epidermal cells and rice protoplasts (confocal microscopy)
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: In Ipomoea trifida, ItfWRKY70 is most highly expressed in leaves compared with stems and roots, and its transcript level is strongly induced by 20% PEG6000 and 100 μM ABA, peaking at 0.5 h (~2.9-fold) after PEG and 3 h (~3.5-fold) after ABA treatment
Plant_Genes.Species: wild diploid Ipomoea trifida (wild relative and probable ancestor of sweet potato)
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 20% PEG6000 applied to I. trifida and to in vitro sweet potato plants to mimic drought; 100 μM ABA treatment of I. trifida plants; soil-based drought stress imposed by withholding water from potted/transplanting-box sweet potato plants for 4–6 weeks; detached-leaf dehydration at 37 °C to assay water loss.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Improving drought tolerance in sweet potato using a WRKY transcription factor from wild Ipomoea trifida via ABA signaling, ROS-scavenging, and stomatal regulation mechanisms
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance (drought tolerance), with effects on water-loss control via stomatal movement and ROS homeostasis under drought stress
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: In I. trifida, ItfWRKY70 transcript levels were induced ~2.91-fold at 0.5 h by 20% PEG6000 and ~3.45-fold at 3 h by 100 μM ABA. In transgenic sweet potato, stomatal apertures in OE-1, OE-2 and OE-3 leaves under drought were reduced by about 50%, 72% and 62%, respectively, compared with WT. ItfWRKY70-overexpressing plants showed significantly greater root length and fresh weight on 20% PEG medium than WT, and higher fresh and dry weight under soil drought, accompanied by significantly higher ABA, proline, SOD and POD levels and significantly lower H2O2 and MDA than WT under drought.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Under non-stress conditions, ItfWRKY70-overexpressing sweet potato lines showed no obvious differences in growth, rooting, or storage root morphology compared with WT. Under drought, OE lines maintained higher leaf water content during dehydration, higher fresh and dry biomass, and improved antioxidant status relative to WT plants.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Ipomoea trifida plants for cloning and native expression analysis; sweet potato (Ipomoea batatas) cultivar Lizixiang transgenic overexpression lines (OE-1 to OE-4) and WT plants; Nicotiana benthamiana leaves for transient expression; rice protoplasts; yeast strain AH109 for transcriptional activation assays
Plant_Genes.Variety: sweet potato cultivar Lizixiang (Ipomoea batatas) used as the transgenic host to characterize ItfWRKY70 function
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Homology-based cloning from I. trifida cDNA; promoter isolation and cis-element analysis; qRT-PCR for tissue-specific and stress-induced expression; subcellular localization using 35S:ItfWRKY70-GFP transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana and rice protoplasts (confocal microscopy); yeast transcriptional activation assays with full-length and truncated ItfWRKY70 in AH109; Agrobacterium-mediated transformation of sweet potato cv. Lizixiang to generate overexpression lines; PEG-induced in vitro drought and soil-based drought assays; physiological measurements of ABA, proline, H2O2, MDA, SOD and POD activities; stomatal aperture imaging and leaf water-loss kinetics; qRT-PCR of ABA biosynthesis, osmolyte, LEA, ROS-scavenging, and stomatal-movement genes
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression of ItfWRKY70 in sweet potato followed by PEG-induced in vitro and soil-based drought tolerance assays with physiological measurements (ABA, proline, ROS, antioxidant enzyme activities, MDA); stomatal aperture and water-loss assays; qRT-PCR of stress-responsive genes; subcellular localization and yeast transcriptional activation assays confirming its function as a nuclear transcriptional activator
Plant_Genes.Field_Trials: Transgenic and WT sweet potato plants (cv. Lizixiang) were grown in greenhouse and field conditions; under normal (non-stress) field conditions, transgenic lines showed no significant morphological differences in aboveground growth or storage roots compared with WT plants.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Cloned from Ipomoea trifida using EST information and reference genomic data combined with homology-based PCR amplification of the full-length cDNA and promoter
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: ItfWRKY70, derived from the drought-resistant wild diploid I. trifida, is a promising genetic resource for engineering or breeding drought-tolerant sweet potato cultivars, as its overexpression enhances drought resistance without obvious growth penalties under normal conditions and improves ABA accumulation, ROS detoxification and water-use efficiency; it may also be useful for improving drought tolerance in other crops via transgenic approaches.
